questions.question: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort algorithm?
questions.explanation: Selection sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part and putting it at the beginning. The time complexity of this algorithm is O(n^2) because for each element, it needs to traverse the rest of the list to find the minimum element.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Bubble Sort algorithm?
options: It is an in-place sorting algorithm
It is not suitable for large datasets
It is used for sorting arrays
questions.explanation: Bubble sort is not suitable for large datasets because its time complexity is O(n^2) and it requires a lot of iterations to sort the list.

questions.question: How does Bubble Sort algorithm work?
options: It compares each pair of adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order
It repeatedly goes through the list and compares each pair of adjacent elements until no more swaps are needed
It starts from the beginning of the list and goes to the end, comparing each element with the next one
It divides the list into two halves and sorts them recursively
questions.explanation: Bubble sort repeatedly goes through the list and compares each pair of adjacent elements until no more swaps are needed. This process continues until the list is sorted.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort algorithm when the input list is already sorted?
questions.explanation: When the input list is already sorted, the best case time complexity of selection sort is O(n), because it only needs to traverse the list once to confirm that it is already sorted.

questions.question: Which algorithm is generally faster than the other when it comes to sorting large datasets?
questions.explanation: Quicksort is generally faster than the other algorithms when it comes to sorting large datasets because it has an average time complexity of O(n log n), which is much faster than the O(n^2) time complexity of selection sort and bubble sort.
